chapter.title: The fine exhortation of a went through his soul. God gave him a change of heart, and fe now

paragraphs.text: CERTAIN HOLY ELDER ON THE WORDS OF THE grasped his colleagne’s feet, saying: ‘It is you who are my lord and LORD'S PRAYER: LEAD US NOT INTO TEMPTATION father’. From that time on, 1here was a strong boad of love between 1 them. <The lowly bishop> said to his clergy: ‘Did I not tell you that One of the saints said: we should triumph, by the grace of God? When there is any ill ‘When we pray to the Lord and say Lead us not into temptation, we feeling between you, do you likewise—and triumph’. The elder also are not saying this so that we shall not be tried; that would be said that a humble man has more glory than the emperor himself; impossible. We are praying not to be overcome by temptation to the for he is only praised in his prescuce, whereas a humble man is extent of doing something displeasing to God. That is what it means praised and said to be blessed both in aad out of his presence. not to enter temptalion. The holy martyrs were tried by their torments but, as they were not overcome by them, they did not enter, 211. CONCERNING AN ELDER into temptation, any more than someone who [lights with a beast OF GREAT VIRTUES WHO GOT and is not devoured by it. When be is devoured, thea he has entered A BROTHER WHO HAD STOLEN THINGS into temptation. So it is with every passion, so Jong as one is not FROM HIM OUT OF PRISON overcome by that passion.
